lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Human. You seem to have found your way into Mistrock. What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Am I not allowed to be here?

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Your kind have managed to establish dominion over the vast majority of these lands. Will you not even let us have our small corner in peace?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that a no?

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Hmph! I can't stop you being here, but I don't have to enjoy your presence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything that I can do to change that?

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: No. Now if this conversation is over, I'm going to have a drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything that I can do to change that? I've already been quite helpful over in Cam Torum.

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: One act of charity will not excuse years of subjugation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Subjugation? That seems like a bit of an over-exaggeration.

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Call it what you will. The end result is the same. Your kind prosper at the expense of ours, and you act like it's not even happening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've seen Cam Torum. You don't seem to be doing too badly.

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: And here you prove my point. One city! One! You see that one city and consider it somehow equal to your many.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well what about this place?

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: A mere mining outpost. It hardly compares to those villas elsewhere on the island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not all humans are the same though. It's not just Cam Torum. I've actually worked with your kind a few times.

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Such as?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well there was this one time when I helped the dwarves of Keldagrim with a bit of a statue issue. Another time I worked with one of the last surviving Imcando dwarves to create a blurite sword.

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Imcando?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! You know about them?

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Of course I know about them! The Imcando were our ancestors, but I thought they were long dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite.

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Hmph! It's still going to take more than that to get me to like you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well if liking is out of the question, how about tolerate?

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Fine. Consider yourself tolerated. Now, did you actually want something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: I'm the overseer for Mistrock. I make sure everything around here is running as it should be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you do that from the pub?

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Am I not allowed a break from time to time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose.

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Hmph!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said the Imcando were your ancestors?

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: That's right. The Lovakengj dwarves were born from the Imcando and we were born from the Lovakengj. If you want to know more, find a historian.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Then be on your way so that I can have a drink.

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: Human. You seem to have returned. Did you want something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything that I can do to change your opinion on me?

lines.speaker: Overseer Khougo
lines.text: No. Now leave me be so that I can have a drink.
